note: WA-PARITY: the SHARED hostile-case corpus that keeps the three fill-boundary seams from ever diverging (cairnx-core verifyFillSpv, cairn-wallet fillspv.ts, cairn public/trade/swapguard.js). Homed here in csd-sdk; each consumer repo vendors a byte-identity-checked copy (the I2 dead-green lesson: the freshness check FAILS, never skips, if a copy drifts). Record-level + seam-agnostic: every seam materializes the SAME scenario in its own injection shape (cairnx-core builds abstract ChainEvents; wallet/site build REAL signed txs + prevout binds), so a wallet-vs-site split goes red. Two families: 'replay' cases exercise the grant/hold/completeness replay that ALL THREE seams share; 'terms' cases exercise the served-offer vs merkle-proven-offer bind that only the CALLERS do (wallet provenTermsMismatch + site verifyOfferContent), because cairnx-core verifyFillSpv takes NO served offer and re-derives everything from proven events.

consts.note: Each adapter MUST assert its own vendored constants equal these before running, so a silent constant drift reds the corpus (not a false green).

scenarios.name: honest-whole-fill
scenarios.desc: The buyer holds a live granted fclaim on an open whole-fill CSD offer, both buried, delivery >= 1. Accepted by every seam (the no-false-refusal baseline).

scenarios.name: honest-partial-fill
scenarios.desc: A partial-enabled offer (min set) with a buyer paying exactly min. Deliverable >= 1, accepted by every seam. Guards against a false-refuse of legitimate partials.

scenarios.name: below-depth-fill
scenarios.desc: The fclaim is not yet buried to requiredClaimDepth. Every seam REFUSES (fail-safe wait, not a burn).

scenarios.name: denied-fclaim
scenarios.desc: B holds fc1 (granted); B posts fc2 while fc1's hold is live -> the resolver DENIES fc2. A lying resolver aliases fc2 as granted. Filling fc2 attests an L0-valid but DELIVERY-LESS target = burn. Every seam re-derives the deny and REFUSES.

scenarios.name: forged-cancel-holder
scenarios.desc: C holds a valid fclaim fcC; B forges holdership and tries to fill fcC. resolve() would reject the payment (who !== claimedBy), burning it. Every seam re-derives that the hold is not mine and REFUSES.

scenarios.name: ocancel-before-grant
scenarios.desc: Seller A posts ocancel {AAA} with no live hold -> the offer is cancelled on-chain and any later fclaim is DENIED. A Byzantine/D2-aliasing resolver still reports the offer open and serves B's fclaim as the granted target. THE F8 CASE: the site includes the ocancel in its scan (swapguard.js) and refuses; the wallet's fillspv.ts must too (the R1 F8 fix). Every seam REFUSES; a wallet-vs-site split here goes red.

scenarios.name: withheld-cancel
scenarios.desc: Seller A posts ocancel {AAA} on-chain BEFORE the fclaim grant, but a lying resolver OMITS the ocancel from the event/hint list it serves (the F8 COMPLETENESS attack, distinct from ocancel-before-grant where the cancel IS in the served set). Each seam must find the cancel via its OWN PoW-verified block-body scan and REFUSE. cairnx-core verifyFillSpv canNOT catch a withheld event - its event set IS the seam's hint list (io.offerEventIds), so completeness is a SEAM obligation the pure layer cannot verify. Hence this scenario is wallet/site-only; the cairnx-core adapter demonstrates the gap by materialising the withheld cancel on-chain-but-excluded-from-hints and asserting verifyFillSpv ACCEPTS (safe), which is exactly why the seams do their own scan.

scenarios.name: payto-strip
scenarios.desc: The open CSD offer is payto-LESS on-chain (recipient = the proven prevout owner A). A read-path attacker strips the funding tx scriptSig (recoverAttester -> null) and serves a /proposal whose want.payto is the attacker. The site + wallet bind the recipient to the MERKLE-PROVEN prevout owner and REFUSE. cairnxCore N/A: verifyFillSpv takes no served offer.

scenarios.name: payto-resign
scenarios.desc: Same payto-less offer, but the attacker RE-SIGNS the funding tx with a throwaway key so recoverAttester returns the attacker (== served proposer). The bind derives the recipient from the merkle-proven PREVOUT OWNER (not the malleable scriptSig), so both seams still REFUSE.

scenarios.name: seller-swap
scenarios.desc: The maker-rebate leg (offer.seller) is swapped to the attacker while want.payto stays honest. resolve() pays the rebate to the on-chain author; a mis-sized rebate leg makes resolve() reject AFTER payment = burn. Both seams bind offer.seller to the proven author and REFUSE.

scenarios.name: spurious-min-whole-fill
scenarios.desc: The offer is a whole-fill open-CSD offer (no on-chain min). A lying resolver ADDS a spurious min -> previewFill flips to the PARTIAL branch (rebate:0) -> requiredFillOutputs drops the maker-rebate leg -> resolve() rejects 'maker rebate unpaid' AFTER payment = full burn (the R1.1 min-bind case). Both seams bind min (present/absent) to the proven record and REFUSE.

scenarios.name: deflated-feeBps
scenarios.desc: A lying resolver serves feeBps=0 for a >= V16 offer (real FEE_BPS_V16=150). requiredFillOutputs then under-sizes/omits the treasury fee leg -> resolve() rejects AFTER payment = burn. Both seams bind feeBps/height/taker/bid to the proven terms and REFUSE.
